product.name: ArGun 260
product.familyDescriptorPl: Akryl barwiony w masie do zastosowań outdoor
product.categorySourceGroup: Durable all weather resistant solution dyed acrylic fabrics for marine applications, automotive, sun blinds, sun umbrella, outdoor furnishing.
product.specLine: 260 g/m², 100% Acrylic, All wheather resistant solution dyed outdoor fabric
descriptions: ArGun 260 is %100 acrylic solution dyed fabric for awnings, marine application, outdoor furnishing, sun umbrella, automotive and sun blinds applications. The fabric is resistant to all wheather conditions and its world's most sun resistant fabric.
It is antibacterial and water repellent.
260 g/m², 100% Acrylic, All wheather resistant solution dyed outdoor fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>1500N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>40N; Weft>60N | EN ISO 13937-4

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Awnings and sun blinds are mentioned but not explicitly framed as architectural or building-system products, so this does not meet the explicit-use threshold.

Properties list 'hi-vis' and norms include EN ISO 20471, but there is no explicit statement of a high-visibility safety garment use; the fabric is otherwise described for awnings, marine, automotive and furnishing applications, so the hi-vis tag appears incidental rather than an intended safety-apparel application.
Antibacterial property is mentioned, but no healthcare or medical use is stated.
Product data explicitly states marine and automotive application use, directly supporting the transport (vehicle/vessel) application.

evidence: for awnings, marine application, outdoor furnishing, sun umbrella, automotive and sun blinds applications
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly described and image-labelled for marine and automotive applications, which directly matches the transport application definition (vehicle/vessel textiles).

evidence: for awnings, marine application, outdoor furnishing, sun umbrella, automotive and sun blinds applications
secondary.rationale: Beyond marine/automotive use, the fabric is explicitly marketed for awnings, sun umbrellas and outdoor furnishing, which are weather-protective outdoor textile uses independent of the transport application.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Awnings and sun blinds are mentioned but not explicitly framed as architectural/building systems, so this does not meet the explicit-use threshold.

Outdoor furnishing is mentioned but this is non-vehicle outdoor furniture use, which is less directly supported than the explicit marine/automotive transport uses and awning/outdoor uses already captured.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Product spans multiple stated end uses (marine, automotive, awnings, sun umbrellas, outdoor furnishing) that do not map cleanly onto a single taxonomy category, requiring human judgment on relative weighting between transport and outdoor/furnishing uses.
